folio.label: 8v
folio.l: And thries had she ben , at Ierusalem
She had passed , many a straunge strem
At Rome had she be , and at Boloyne
In Galice at Seynt Iamez , and at Coloyne
She coude meche , of wandryng´ by the weye
Gat tothed was she , sothly to seye
Vp on an aumblere , esily she sat
Wel I wympled , and on hire hed an hat´
As brod as is , a bokeler or a targe
A fote mantel , aboute hire hepes large
And on hire fete , a peire of spores sharpe
In felashippe , coude she , laughe & carpe
The remedies of loue , she coude ¹ chaunce
For of æat art , she knew æe olde daunce
A Good man æäe was , of religion
That was , a Persou , of a poreof a pore , a Persou~a b Persou~ , of a a pore toun

note.notetxt: Persou480 A letter b is written above the word Persou , and a letter a above the word pore to indicate the correct word order. I am not sure whether it was done by the scribe as the ink is a bit lighter. Moreover, the inversion does not meke too much sense, the problem being the clouster to be revertedODR

folio.l: But riche he was , of holy thou©t and werke
He was also , a lerned man , a clerke
That cristes gospel , trewely wold preche
His ¹isshyns deuoutly , wold he teche
Benygne he was , and wonder diligent
And in aduäsite , ful a bidyng , and pacient
For whicú he was preued , often sithes
Ful loth were hi~ to curse , for hise tithes
But rather wold he ©euen , w oute doute
Vn to his pore parisshyns , a boute
Of his offryng´ , and eke of his substance
He coude in litil , thyng´ , haue suffisance
Wyde was his ¹issú , and houses fer a sonder
But he left nou©t , for reyn ne for thonder
In sekenesse and in myschef , to visite
The ferthest , in his ¹issú , moche and lite
Vp on his feet , and in his hond a staf´
This noble ensaumple , to his shepe he ©af´
That first he wrou©t , and afterward he taugút
Ou©t of æe gospel , he tho wordes caugút
And this figåe , he added eke ther to
That if gold rust , what shuld Iren do
For if a preest be foul , on whom we trust
No wonder is , a lewed man to rust
And shame it is , if a preest take kepe
To se a sheton shepherde , & a clene shepe
